items.meaning: to remove clothing; (of aircraft) to leave the ground
items.example: The plane will take _____ at 3pm from Heathrow airport.

items.meaning: to begin a hobby or activity; to occupy time or space
items.example: I've decided to take _____ tennis as a hobby this year.

items.meaning: to accept responsibility; to employ someone
items.example: She took _____ a lot of responsibility in her new job.

items.meaning: to gain control of something; to begin someone else's job
items.example: The new manager will take _____ from Mr. Smith next month.

items.meaning: to resemble a parent or relative in appearance or character
items.example: He takes _____ his father — they look almost identical.

items.meaning: to return something; to withdraw a statement or promise
items.example: I'd like to take _____ what I said earlier — I was wrong.

items.meaning: to remove something; to invite someone to go somewhere socially
items.example: Let me take you _____ for dinner to celebrate your promotion.

items.meaning: to provide shelter; to understand; to deceive
items.example: The refugees were taken _____ by local families during the crisis.

items.meaning: to remove or subtract; to buy food to eat elsewhere
items.example: The police took the suspect _____ for questioning yesterday.

items.meaning: to write information; to remove something from a wall or high place
items.example: Please take _____ these important points in your notebook.

items.meaning: to continue; to happen; to talk at great length
items.example: The meeting went _____ for three hours without a break.

items.meaning: to leave home for social reasons; to stop burning
items.example: Would you like to go _____ for dinner this weekend?

items.meaning: to explode; to ring (alarm); to become bad (food)
items.example: The bomb could go _____ at any moment according to experts.

items.meaning: to experience something difficult; to examine carefully
items.example: Let's go _____ the plan one more time before the presentation.

items.meaning: to review or examine; to be received in a particular way
items.example: Can you go _____ my essay and check for grammar mistakes?

items.meaning: to increase; to be built or erected
items.example: House prices have gone _____ significantly in the last year.

items.meaning: to return to a place or previous state
items.example: We can't go _____ on our promise to help them move house.

items.meaning: to proceed with something; to take place as planned
items.example: Despite the difficulties, we decided to go _____ with the project.

items.meaning: to decrease; to sink; to be remembered in a particular way
items.example: The Titanic went _____ after hitting an iceberg in 1912.

items.meaning: to leave a place; to disappear
items.example: I wish this headache would go _____ — I've had it all day.

items.meaning: to take care of someone or something
items.example: Can you look _____ my cat while I'm on holiday next week?

items.meaning: to search for something or someone
items.example: I've been looking _____ my keys everywhere but can't find them.

items.meaning: to anticipate something with pleasure
items.example: We're really looking _____ the summer holidays this year.

items.meaning: to investigate or examine carefully
items.example: Police are looking _____ the cause of the mysterious fire.

items.meaning: to be careful; to watch for danger
items.example: Look _____! There's a car coming towards you very fast!

items.meaning: to search for information in a reference book; to improve
items.example: If you don't know a word, look it _____ in the dictionary.

items.meaning: to consider someone or something as inferior
items.example: She tends to look _____ people who didn't go to university.

items.meaning: to admire and respect someone greatly
items.example: Young children often look _____ their older siblings and copy them.

items.meaning: to examine or inspect quickly
items.example: Can you look _____ my homework before I hand it in tomorrow?

items.meaning: to think about the past; to remember an earlier time
items.example: When I look _____ on my childhood, I realize how happy I was.

items.meaning: to rise from bed; to stand up
items.example: I always get _____ at 6:30am to go jogging before breakfast.

items.meaning: to board transport; to make progress; to have a good relationship
items.example: How are you getting _____ with your new course at university?

items.meaning: to leave transport; to escape punishment
items.example: Please get _____ the bus at the next stop near the hospital.

items.meaning: to recover from illness or disappointment
items.example: It took me weeks to get _____ the flu I caught last month.

items.meaning: to complete successfully; to make contact by phone
items.example: How will we get _____ all this work before the deadline?

items.meaning: to escape; to go on holiday
items.example: The thieves managed to get _____ before the police arrived.

items.meaning: to return; to retrieve something taken from you
items.example: What time did you get _____ from your trip to London yesterday?

items.meaning: to manage with limited resources or difficulty
items.example: We don't earn much but we manage to get _____ somehow.

items.meaning: to have a good relationship with someone
items.example: I get _____ really well with my new flatmates — we're good friends.

items.meaning: to make someone feel depressed; to descend
items.example: Don't let exam stress get you _____ — just do your best.

items.meaning: to continue at the same rate; to maintain standards
items.example: Well done! Keep _____ the good work and you'll pass the exam.

items.meaning: to continue doing something repeatedly
items.example: The rain kept _____ falling all day without stopping once.

items.meaning: to stay away from; to avoid something
items.example: Keep _____ the grass! There's a sign saying not to walk on it.

items.meaning: to remain at a distance; to prevent from approaching
items.example: Try to keep _____ from junk food if you want to lose weight.

items.meaning: to prevent from approaching; to withhold information
items.example: The police kept the crowds _____ while the President's car passed.

items.meaning: to remain outside; to prevent from entering
items.example: Please keep _____ of the kitchen while I'm cooking.

items.meaning: to stay within limits; to fulfill a promise or arrangement
items.example: You must keep _____ your promise to help with the school concert.

items.meaning: to control or limit; to remain at a low level
items.example: The doctor advised him to keep _____ his cholesterol levels.

items.meaning: to not reveal information; to confine indoors
items.example: They decided to keep the news _____ until after the wedding.

items.meaning: to persist with something; to continue working despite difficulty
items.example: She kept _____ me for hours with her endless complaints.

items.meaning: to postpone; to cause someone to lose interest or enthusiasm
items.example: Don't put _____ until tomorrow what you can do today!

items.meaning: to wear clothing; to switch on; to pretend or perform
items.example: Put _____ your coat — it's freezing outside this morning.

items.meaning: to provide accommodation; to erect or display
items.example: We can put you _____ for the night if you miss your train.

items.meaning: to store in the proper place; to save money for later
items.example: Please put _____ your toys when you've finished playing.

items.meaning: to place on a surface; to criticize; to record in writing
items.example: The teacher had to put the phone _____ during the lesson.

items.meaning: to extinguish a fire; to inconvenience someone
items.example: Firefighters managed to put _____ the blaze before it spread.

items.meaning: to return to original position; to postpone to a later time
items.example: Can you put _____ the meeting for an hour? I'm running late.

items.meaning: to propose or suggest an idea or plan formally
items.example: She put _____ an interesting idea at the conference yesterday.

items.meaning: to connect by telephone; to cause someone to experience something
items.example: Could you put me _____ to the manager, please? It's urgent.

items.meaning: to tolerate something unpleasant without complaining
items.example: I don't know how she puts _____ his rude behavior for so long.

items.meaning: to switch on a device; to attack suddenly
items.example: Can you turn _____ the lights when you come in? It's dark.

items.meaning: to switch off; to cause someone to lose interest
items.example: Don't forget to turn _____ your computer before you leave.

items.meaning: to arrive; to increase volume; to be found unexpectedly
items.example: Could you turn _____ the volume? I can't hear the TV properly.

items.meaning: to refuse an offer; to reduce volume or heat
items.example: Please turn _____ the music — the neighbors are complaining!

items.meaning: to result or happen in a particular way; to attend an event
items.example: Over 500 people turned _____ for the auditions for the play.

items.meaning: to transform or change into something completely different
items.example: Water turns _____ ice when the temperature drops below zero.

items.meaning: to flip to the other side; to hand over to authorities
items.example: Remember to turn _____ your exam paper when the time is up.

items.meaning: to return the way you came; to send back
items.example: It's too late to turn _____ now — we're already committed.

items.meaning: to refuse entry; to look in a different direction
items.example: The hotel had to turn guests _____ because they were fully booked.

items.meaning: to face the opposite direction; to improve a bad situation
items.example: Turn _____ and face me when I'm talking to you!

items.meaning: to stop functioning; to collapse emotionally; to analyze into parts
items.example: My car broke _____ on the motorway and I had to call for help.

items.meaning: to end a relationship; to divide into smaller pieces
items.example: The couple broke _____ after twenty years of marriage.

items.meaning: to escape; to start suddenly (war, disease, fire)
items.example: A fire broke _____ in the kitchen early this morning.

items.meaning: to enter illegally; to interrupt a conversation
items.example: Thieves broke _____ to the shop during the night.

items.meaning: to end abruptly; to become detached from the main part
items.example: She broke _____ the conversation and walked away angrily.

items.meaning: to make an important discovery; to force a way through an obstacle
items.example: Scientists have broken _____ in their research on the disease.

items.meaning: to enter illegally; to suddenly begin laughing or singing
items.example: Someone broke _____ our house while we were on holiday.

items.meaning: to escape from control; to separate from a larger group
items.example: The prisoner broke _____ from the guards and ran.

items.meaning: to make neither profit nor loss; to reach the point of balance
items.example: It took two years for the business to break _____.

items.meaning: to end a relationship or tradition completely
items.example: He decided to break _____ tradition and serve pizza at Christmas.

items.meaning: to stop trying; to surrender; to quit a habit
items.example: Don't give _____ trying — you can learn anything with practice!

items.meaning: to surrender; to submit work to a teacher; to yield to pressure
items.example: After arguing for hours, he finally gave _____ and admitted I was right.

items.meaning: to donate freely; to reveal a secret or answer unintentionally
items.example: She decided to give _____ her old clothes to charity.

items.meaning: to return something to its owner
items.example: Could you give _____ the book I lent you last month?

items.meaning: to distribute to a group; to stop functioning; to emit
items.example: The teacher gave _____ the exam papers at the start of the lesson.

items.meaning: to emit a smell, heat, or light
items.example: These flowers give _____ a beautiful scent in the morning.

items.meaning: to stop believing in someone's ability to succeed
items.example: Don't give _____ yourself — your teacher still believes in you.

items.meaning: to stop doing something annoying; to hand control to someone
items.example: The presenter gave _____ to the next speaker after ten minutes.

items.meaning: to cause or lead to something (often a problem or situation)
items.example: The new policy gave _____ a great deal of public controversy.

items.meaning: to collapse; to yield; to be replaced by something
items.example: The old bridge finally gave _____ under the weight of the lorry.

items.meaning: to establish or arrange; to prepare equipment for use
items.example: They're planning to set _____ a new business in the city centre.

items.meaning: to begin a journey; to cause to explode; to trigger an alarm
items.example: What time do you want to set _____ for the airport tomorrow?

items.meaning: to begin a journey; to intend to do something; to arrange systematically
items.example: We set _____ to solve the mystery but found no evidence at all.

items.meaning: to begin doing something with energy and purpose
items.example: She set _____ reorganizing the office as soon as she arrived.

items.meaning: to delay progress; to cost a particular amount of money
items.example: The unexpected repairs set the project _____ by three weeks.

items.meaning: to record in writing; to establish rules or principles formally
items.example: The committee set _____ clear guidelines for all future events.

items.meaning: to begin and seem likely to continue for some time
items.example: If we don't act quickly, bad habits will set _____.

items.meaning: to save or reserve for a specific purpose; to disregard temporarily
items.example: Try to set _____ at least one hour a day for focused study.

items.meaning: to make someone or something noticeably different or special
items.example: Her confidence and clear ideas set her _____ from the other candidates.

items.meaning: to state or explain formally; to begin a journey (literary)
items.example: The report sets _____ the main findings of the two-year study.
